The '''59th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], took place on 19 February 2006 at the [[Odeon Luxe Leicester Square|Odeon Leicester Square]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2005 in film|2005]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2005.

''[[Brokeback Mountain]]'' won [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]], Best Director for [[Ang Lee]], Best Supporting Actor for [[Jake Gyllenhaal]], and Best Adapted Screenplay.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/4724066.stm |title=Brokeback emerges as Bafta winner |publisher=[[BBC News]] |date=19 February 2006 |access-date=25 June 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.indiewire.com/2006/02/brokeback-mountain-wins-4-bafta-awards-including-best-picture-77139/ |title="Brokeback Mountain" Wins 4 BAFTA Awards, Including Best Picture |first=Eugene |last=Hernandez |publisher=[[IndieWire]] |date=20 February 2006 |access-date=25 June 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a627188/6-times-the-baftas-defied-the-oscars-brokeback-mountain-ben-affleck/ |title=6 times the BAFTAs defied the Oscars: Brokeback Mountain, Ben Affleck |first=Emma |last=Dibdin |publisher=[[Digital Spy]] |date=8 February 2015 |access-date=25 June 2022}}</ref> [[Philip Seymour Hoffman]] won Best Actor for ''[[Capote (film)|Capote]]'' and [[Reese Witherspoon]] won Best Actress for ''[[Walk the Line]]''. ''[[The Constant Gardener (film)|The Constant Gardener]]'' received the most nominations with 10;<ref>{{cite web |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/4626932.stm |title=Bafta 2006 nominations in full |publisher=[[BBC News]] |date=19 January 2006 |access-date=25 June 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2006/jan/19/awardsandprizes.johnlecarre |title=The Constant Gardener reaps Bafta nominations |work=[[The Guardian]] |date=19 January 2006 |access-date=25 June 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.empireonline.com/movies/news/constant-gardener-rakes-bafta-noms/ |title=Constant Gardener Rakes In Bafta Noms |first=Willow |last=Green |work=[[Empire (magazine)|Empire]] |date=19 January 2006 |access-date=25 June 2022}}</ref> the film only received one award: Best Editing for [[Claire Simpson]]. ''[[Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit]]'', directed by [[Nick Park]] and [[Steve Box]], was voted Outstanding British Film of 2005.

[[Stephen Fry]] hosted the ceremony for the fifth consecutive year.
